cases.response_preview: These four inner planets are rocky because they formed close to the Sun, where temperatures were too high for ices like water and ammonia to condense. As a result, only silicate rocks could form in their cores.

body.npc_response: These four inner planets are rocky because they formed close to the Sun, where temperatures were too high for ices like water and ammonia to condense. As a result, only silicate rocks could form in their cores.

cases.response_preview: An astronomical unit, or AU for short, is a standard measurement of distance used to compare the vast scales within our Solar System. One AU equals about 93 million miles (149.6 million kilometers), which is roughly the average distance between Earth and the Sun when both planets are aligned on opposite sides of the Sun -

body.npc_response: An astronomical unit, or AU for short, is a standard measurement of distance used to compare the vast scales within our Solar System. One AU equals about 93 million miles (149.6 million kilometers), which is roughly the average distance between Earth and the Sun when both planets are aligned on opposite sides of the Sun -

cases.response_preview: Asteroids and comets are remnants from the early Solar System, providing clues about its formation. They contain primitive materials that have been preserved since the planetesimal era, offering insights into the conditions of the inner Solar System during planetary accretion.

body.npc_response: Asteroids and comets are remnants from the early Solar System, providing clues about its formation. They contain primitive materials that have been preserved since the planetesimal era, offering insights into the conditions of the inner Solar System during planetary accretion.
